narrative.headline: 2022 Volkswagen Jetta: brakes lead the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 40 complaints with NHTSA. Service brakes is the most reported area at 30%, followed closely by the engine (28%) and electrical system (25%). Driver-assist features round out the list: forward collision avoidance (20%) and lane departure (18%). None of these reports involve a crash, fire, injury, or death.

issues.sample: The contact owns a 2022 Volkswagen Jetta. The contact stated when he released the hill start assist the brakes seized, causing the vehicle to not move. The contact stated that after several minutes the brakes released. The dealer was contacted and stated that the feature operated
issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: The single most reported area, with 12 complaints (30%). No crashes, fires, or injuries are reported.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 11 complaints (28%), nearly even with the brakes. No crashes, fires, or injuries are reported.

issues.blurb: 8 complaints (20%) about this driver-assist feature. No crashes, fires, or injuries are reported.
